context_note: Sweden vs Tunisia - Sweden is a clear favorite (FTR 52.1% / Draw 27.95% / Tunisia 20.0%, vig-adj). Draw price is high (same RULE8 trigger as JPN-NED, 27.9%), but the favorite-underdog gap (32pp) is bigger than JPN-NED's (21pp) - closer to the GER-CUR/QAT-SUI 'underdog gets outclassed' regime than JPN-NED's 'even terms' regime. Applied refined RULE8 distinction (game_dynamics_analysis.md): direction of count-prop estimates kept strong where the underdog-outclassed pattern applies (Q2), shrunk where ESPN-vs-market signals conflict (Q1).

q1_tunisia_more_corners_than_sweden_ht.method: ESPN raw (Tunisia avg 3.8 vs Sweden 2.6 corners, 1H-scaled lambdas 1.9/1.3, continuity-corrected Skellam) gives ~0.52 for 'Tunisia>Sweden'. But full-match corner-handicap market says the OPPOSITE: Sweden>Tunisia 61.5% vs Tunisia>=Sweden only 38.5% - a large conflict. Trusted the market for THIS specific matchup (Sweden a clear favorite, likely to dominate territory vs Tunisia specifically; Tunisia's 3.8 avg is inflated by an 11-corner game vs Mali, a much weaker side). Shaded below the tie-inclusive 38.5% for HT tie-proneness and the strict '>' requirement.

q2_sweden_more_fouls_than_tunisia.method: Skellam on historical foul rates: Sweden avg=8.6 (n=5, range 6-15), Tunisia avg=15.4 (n=5, range 9-29) - large, consistent gap (mean_diff=-6.8, raw P=0.08). This is the GER-CUR/QAT-SUI 'favorite fouls less / outclassed underdog fouls more' pattern (RULE7-consistent), NOT the JPN-NED pattern (Japan played on even terms, fouls converged to exact tie). Sweden's favorite-gap here (32pp) is larger than JPN-NED's (21pp), supporting the outclassed-underdog read. Modest shrinkage 0.08->0.15 for RULE8 general caution given the still-high draw price (27.95%), but direction kept strong.

q3_2h_more_goals_than_1h.method: Direct from Smarkets 'Highest scoring half' market, SECOND_HALF contract, vig-adjusted (FIRST_HALF 28.95% / EQUAL 28.78% / SECOND_HALF 42.27%). Undeviated.

q4_sweden_scores_first_and_tunisia_score.method: Naive independence product: P(Sweden scores first)=57.85% (vig-adj 'Team to score first goal' market) x P(Tunisia scores 1+ in 2H)=38.67% (vig-adj '2H Tunisia O/U 0.5 goals') = 0.224. Per USA-PAR Q2 (similar compound, naive product 0.22 was TOO LOW vs actual YES/crowd 0.31 - the 'blowout consolation goal' dynamic: a dominant favorite scoring first often leads to a one-sided 2H where the trailing underdog gets a late/garbage-time goal). Nudged up slightly to account for this.

q5_tunisia_more_2h_sot_than_sweden.method: ESPN full-match SOT: Tunisia avg=2.8 (n=5) vs Sweden avg=4.2 (n=5). 2H-scaled Skellam (lambda_Tunisia=1.4, lambda_Sweden=2.1), continuity-corrected for strict inequality: P(Tunisia>Sweden)=0.26. Submitted near-raw per RULE7 (unshaded first-pass models win).

q6_penalty_or_red_card.method: P(A or B) = P(A)+P(B)-P(A)*P(B). P(penalty)=28.57% (offer-only illiquid, x0.945 ratio -> 0.27). P(red card)=14.29% (liquid, 'Any player sent off'). P(A or B)=0.27+0.1429-(0.27*0.1429)=0.374.

q7_sweden_win.method: Direct from Smarkets full-time-result market, vig-adjusted (Sweden 52.05%). Treated as tier1_market undeviated per established convention.

q8_3plus_total_goals.method: Direct from Smarkets 'Over/under 2.5 goals' market, OVER contract = 3+ goals, vig-adjusted 42.62%. Undeviated.

q9_gyokeres_score_or_assist.method: Direct from Smarkets 'Player to score or assist' market, Viktor Gyokeres contract resolved cleanly (name field on contract object - the CIV-ECU player-ID resolution issue was a str/int key bug, now fixed). Offer-only 52.08% (illiquid, no bid), x0.945 ratio -> 0.492. Sweden's focal striker in a match they're favored to win comfortably.

q10_skhiri_1plus_sot.method: Direct from Smarkets 'Player to have over 0.5 shots on target' market, Ellyes Skhiri contract resolved cleanly. LIQUID two-sided quote (bid 14.71% / offer 16.95%, mid 15.83%). Undeviated - Skhiri is Tunisia's defensive midfielder, low shot-volume role consistent with a low SOT line.

questions.leaderboard_question: At halftime, will Tunisia have more corner kicks than Sweden?
questions.note: WON by trusting the market over Tunisia's ESPN average. Full-match corners ended Sweden 4 - Tunisia 2, the SAME pattern as the Q2 foul miss (Tunisia's historical corner avg of 3.8 did not transfer to this match) - but here the corner-handicap market (Sweden favored 61.5%) had already priced that in, and we deferred to it.

questions.leaderboard_question: Will Sweden commit more fouls than Tunisia?
questions.note: OUR 3RD-WORST RESULT EVER. Outcome=YES: Sweden 10 fouls > Tunisia 8. Sweden's 10 is close to their own n=5 average (8.6) - Sweden played roughly true to form. Tunisia's 8 is WAY below their n=5 average (15.4, range 9-29) - Tunisia's historical foul rate did NOT transfer to this match. Sweden won 5-1 (a blowout); Tunisia, on the wrong end of it, fouled far less than usual (disengagement/'blowout fatigue', same mechanism as GER-CUR's Curacao going 19min without a foul after falling further behind). See RULE10 in project_crowd_bias_model.md - this is now 2/2 (GER-CUR, SWE-TUN): in BLOWOUTS, the WINNING team fouls >= the LOSING team, regardless of which team's historical average was higher.

questions.leaderboard_question: Will the second half have more total goals than the first half?
questions.note: 1H had 3 goals (7',30',43'), 2H had 3 goals (59',84',90+6') - EQUAL, not '2H more than 1H'. tier1_market undeviated won.

questions.leaderboard_question: Will Sweden score the first goal of the game and Tunisia score in the second half?
questions.note: Sweden scored first (7') TRUE, but Tunisia's only goal (Rekik, 43') was in 1H not 2H - AND=FALSE. Naive independence product (0.224->0.24) beat crowd.

questions.leaderboard_question: Will Tunisia have more shots on target than Sweden in the second half?
questions.note: Sweden SOT=7 (3 2H goals among them), Tunisia SOT=2 total. Unshaded first-pass Skellam (RULE7) correct again.

questions.leaderboard_question: Will a penalty kick be awarded OR a red card be shown in the match?
questions.note: Only 1 yellow card in the whole match, no penalty. Low estimate (0.21, illiquid-ratio-adjusted) won big.
Sweden won 5-1 (much bigger than the 52% market implied). Crowd's 59% was closer. Small loss, same pattern as other tier1_market match-winner deferrals.

questions.leaderboard_question: Will the match have 3 or more total goals?
questions.note: 6 total goals (way more than '3+'), but both us and crowd were <50% - tiny win on a near-tie.

questions.leaderboard_question: Will Viktor Gyokeres score or assist a goal (excluding own goals)?
questions.note: Gyokeres scored at 59' (Sweden's 3rd goal). Direct market resolution paid off.

questions.leaderboard_question: Will Ellyes Skhiri have at least 1 shot on target?
questions.note: Tunisia's total SOT=2 (neither apparently Skhiri's). Liquid market price (15.83%) much closer than crowd's 31%.

post_match_results.summary: Sweden won 5-1, a blowout well beyond the 52% pre-match favorite price. 8/10 beat crowd, total +17.37 - a strong match overall, EXCEPT for Q2 (Sweden more fouls than Tunisia, -33.9, our 3rd-worst-ever single result). Q2's failure mechanism: Tunisia's strong, consistent historical foul average (15.4, range 9-29 across n=5) completely failed to transfer to this match - Tunisia fouled only 8 (Sweden, playing near its own average of 8.6, fouled 10). This is the SAME mechanism as GER-CUR's Q3 win (Curacao fouled below their implied rate after falling further behind) but in REVERSE: there, the underdog's LOW relative foul rate matched our (already-low) estimate; here, the underdog's (Tunisia's) historical foul rate was HIGH, but the blowout collapsed it anyway, inverting our estimate's direction entirely. New RULE10: in BLOWOUTS, the WINNING team fouls >= the LOSING team (2/2: GER-CUR Germany 18>Curacao 11; SWE-TUN Sweden 10>Tunisia 8), independent of either team's historical average. Q1 (corners, same Tunisia-average-doesn't-transfer pattern) WON because a market existed and we deferred to it instead of the ESPN average - Q2 had no such market to fall back on.
